sections.paragraph:
εὐπραγέω 1 aor. εὐπράγησα (=εὖ πράσσω, s. πρᾶγμα; Thu. et al., mostly=‘be well off’; so also Jos., Ant. 13, 284; TestGad 4:5) do what is right (cp. Ps 35:4 Sym.) 2 Cl 17:7.—DELG s.v. πράσσω.

aor. aor. = aorist

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 410
